name: Ann Kathleen Mckay's Strawberry Cake a La Dacquoise
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Eau de Vie Framboise (raspberry brandy)
12 Fraise des bois with stems (wild strawberries, optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut one of the 9-inch genoise cakes into two layers. These will be the top two layers of the cake, while the whole genoise will serve as the bottom layer.
2. Sprinkle each genoise layer lightly with 2 tablespoons of Framboise.
3. Flavor 2 cups of buttercream with 1 tablespoon of Framboise.
4. Whip creme fraiche and heavy cream together to form stiff peaks. Add 1/4 cup of creme anglaise and 1 tablespoon Framboise, then continue whipping until stiff peaks form again.
5. If the strawberries are small, slice them lengthwise; if larger, cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices.
6. Spread one-third of the buttercream on the bottom genoise layer. Arrange sliced berries around the perimeter with the cut side out. Continue covering the bottom with half the berries and spread with half the whipped cream-creme anglaise mixture.
7. Repeat steps 5-6 for the second genoise layer, then place the final layer on top and spread with a thin layer of buttercream, smoothing it nicely.
8. Use a small star piping tip to create an attractive border around the top of the cake with the remaining buttercream (optional food coloring can be added).
9. Arrange Fraise des bois along the perimeter and garnish with strawberry leaves and flowers.
10. Refrigerate for 2-4 hours before serving.
Preparation time: 1 1/2 hours
